This work was originally booked for 13th of March 2026 and was cancelled due to the work needing to be rescheduled.

This work is taking place on London Road.

Steps.Message:
Good morning, our Customer Champions are on site today for any support.
 
In addition to this, specialist electricians, Haste are continuing on site today to investigate the damage that has occurred within some properties due to the complex fault.
 
If you have had damage within your property and you have not made our team aware of this, please contact our Customer Champions who are located in the unit outside 180 Shernall Street or call us on 105.
 
We appreciate your continued patience whilst we work on having every property restored.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 11:59 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our underground* cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the NR17 1, NR17 2, NR18 9, NR9 4 area to investigate the power cut affecting 308 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 12:27 - Our engineers have restored power to all 909 properties in the Tunbridge Wells TN4 0 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have managed to remotely divert power around the underground network fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hello, Kevin from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today. 

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently, and although we don't believe today's issue to be related, we are sorry for further disruption. Power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers, but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

You may not have had other power cuts recently, but if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations. 

Our staff are still working to get your power back on as quickly as they can, and we'll update you again soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Kevin at 12:04 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is on our underground network that initially interrupted power to 909 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 19 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 14:00 and 15:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We   have staff in the area beginning their investigations and we're alsp working to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineers will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about all we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the best information we have.
